skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Build polished frontend interfaces with distinctive visual direction and production-ready implementation guidance.
skill.author: CesarAugustusGroB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a license URL and several prose keywords as possible network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found only SKILL.md design guidance and Apache license text, with no executable code, commands, credential access, network calls, or prompt injection attempts. The flagged items are false positives and the skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Many AI-generated interfaces look generic and lack visual intent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with stronger typography, layout, motion, and theme choices.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of web components, pages, applications, and interfaces.
Encourages a clear aesthetic direction before implementation begins.
Specifies attention to typography, color, motion, spatial composition, and visual details.
Supports implementation planning for HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend stacks.
Pushes generated interfaces away from common generic AI design patterns.
limitations: Does not include reusable code assets, component libraries, or design tokens.
Does not run accessibility, performance, or browser compatibility checks by itself.
Depends on the host AI model and project context for final implementation quality.
May require user constraints to avoid overly elaborate visual directions.

use_cases.title: Design a Memorable Landing Page
use_cases.description: Turn a product brief into a visually distinctive page with a clear theme, layout, typography, and motion direction.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Interface
use_cases.description: Refine a generic component or page so it feels more intentional, polished, and suited to its audience.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Creative Web App
use_cases.description: Create an interface concept with stronger visual identity while still producing functional frontend code.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Simple Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete frontend screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a responsive landing page for [product]. Use a distinctive aesthetic direction, production-ready HTML and CSS, and polished visual details.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this [component] for [audience]. Keep the behavior the same, but improve layout, typography, color, spacing, and interaction states.

prompt_templates.title: Create an App Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a frontend interface for [app idea]. Include key workflows, responsive states, strong visual identity, and implementation-ready component structure.

prompt_templates.title: Define and Implement a Visual System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for cohesive design and code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose a bold visual direction for [domain], define the interface principles, then implement the main screen with cohesive typography, color, motion, and layout.

output_examples.input: Create a dashboard for a boutique fitness studio.
output_examples.output: A compact operational dashboard with editorial typography, warm accent colors, session cards, attendance trends, and refined hover states.

output_examples.input: Make this pricing page less generic.
output: A clearer visual concept tied to the product category.
More distinctive plan hierarchy, button states, and comparison layout.
Improved spacing, typography pairing, and color contrast.

best_practices: Provide the product context, target audience, and required frontend framework.
Name accessibility, responsiveness, or performance constraints before implementation.
Ask for a clear aesthetic direction when the first concept feels too generic.
anti_patterns: Do not request vague beauty improvements without explaining the audience or purpose.
Do not accept decorative effects that harm readability or usability.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for manual accessibility and browser testing.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components, pages, applications, and interfaces with stronger visual direction and implementation detail.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains written guidance and a license file only.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it only for landing pages?
faq.answer: No. It can guide components, full pages, web apps, and other frontend interfaces.

faq.question: Will it guarantee accessible output?
faq.answer: No. It can guide design choices, but accessibility still needs explicit requirements and testing.

faq.question: Why were static risks dismissed?
faq.answer: The flagged terms appear in prose and Apache license text, not in executable code or network logic.
